question_text: How is ARIMA different from exponential smoothing?
source_refs:
ARIMA models provide another approach to time series forecasting.
Exponential smoothing and ARIMA models are the two most widely-used
approaches to time series forecasting, and provide complementary
approaches to the problem. While exponential smoothing models are
based on a description of the trend and seasonality in the data,
ARIMA models aim to describe the autocorrelations in the data.

question_text: When would ARIMA be a better choice than exponential smoothing?
source_refs:

question_text: Why do we compare a forecasting model against benchmark methods?
source_refs:
In this chapter, you will learn general tools that are useful for
many different forecasting situations. It will describe some methods
for benchmark forecasting, methods for checking whether a
forecasting method has adequately utilized the available
information, and methods for measuring forecast accuracy. Each of
the tools discussed in this chapter will be used repeatedly in
subsequent chapters as you develop and explore a range of
forecasting methods.

question_text: How do I know when a benchmark method is no longer enough on its own?
source_refs:
In this chapter, you will learn general tools that are useful for
many different forecasting situations. It will describe some methods
for benchmark forecasting, methods for checking whether a
forecasting method has adequately utilized the available

question_text: Why do more recent observations get more weight in exponential smoothing?
source_refs:
Forecasts produced using exponential smoothing methods are weighted
averages of past observations, with the weights decaying
exponentially as the observations get older. In other words, the
more recent the observation, the higher the associated weight. This
framework generates reliable forecasts quickly and for a wide range
of time series, which is a great advantage and of major importance
to applications in business.

question_text: When is exponential smoothing a poor fit for the data?
source_refs:

question_text: How do I know when a time series plot is revealing something my forecasting method needs to capture?
source_refs:
The first thing to do in any data analysis task is to plot the data.
Graphs enable many features of the data to be visualized, including
patterns, unusual observations, and changes over time. The features
that are seen in plots of the data must then be incorporated, as far
as possible, into the forecasting methods to be used.

question_text: What is the difference between a univariate and a multivariate time series?
source_refs:
What to do if you have to deal with multivariate time series? In
this chapter, you will learn how to identify patterns in the
distribution, central tendency and spread over pairs or groups of
data.

question_text: How would I know that I am missing an important relationship by only looking at one series at a time?
source_refs:
What to do if you have to deal with multivariate time series? In
this chapter, you will learn how to identify patterns in the
distribution, central tendency and spread over pairs or groups of
data.

question_text: How would I know when a univariate view is not enough for the question I want to answer?
source_refs:
Univariate plots are designed to learn as much as possible about the
distribution, central tendency and spread of the data at hand. In
this chapter you will be presented with some visual tools used to
diagnose univariate times series.

question_text: What could go wrong if I analyze the full time range when I really need a specific period?
source_refs:
In this chapter, you'll expand your time series data library to
include weather data in the Boston area. Before you can conduct any
analysis, you'll need to do some data manipulation, including
merging multiple xts objects and isolating certain periods of the
data. It's a great opportunity for more practice!

question_text: Why would it be a mistake to analyze the weather data before merging the relevant xts objects?
source_refs:

question_text: Why isn't sports data enough on its own?
source_refs:
Having exhausted other options, your client now believes Boston's
tourism industry must be related to the success of local sports
teams. In your final task on this project, your supervisor has asked
you to assemble some time series data on Boston's sports teams over
the past few years.

question_text: How would my use of sports data change in a different real-world context?
source_refs:
Having exhausted other options, your client now believes Boston's
tourism industry must be related to the success of local sports
teams. In your final task on this project, your supervisor has asked
you to assemble some time series data on Boston's sports teams over
the past few years.
